title: Running a pod from a generated .yml file causes errors and high cpu load
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

A pod run via generated by podman .yml file causes errors and high cpu usage, while running it manually does not produce such issues.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a pod and attach a container (which runs a some kind of web service, I guess)

2. Generate an .yml file with `podman generate kube` command

3. Kill the original pod, run it via `podman play kube` command, observe logs and cpu usage

**Describe the results you received:**
Podman is running in rootless mode.
In my case deluge torrent client is affected, but I confirmed the bug with other container that provides web service.
Creating the pod and attaching a container to it works fine, however running it via generated by podman yml file causes deluge to generate a lot of output in podman logs and in their own log files, and it hammers two cores of my Raspberry Pi 4. Running more cointainers like that hammers all cores of my RPi. This only happens when pod and containers are run via `podman play kube` command. `htop` shows the container causing the high cpu load.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Running a pod with containers attached that does not cause a high cpu load and lot of logs output.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Here I will provide all my steps to reproduce the issue. The container I use for this is https://hub.docker.com/r/linuxserver/deluge.
The `podman logs` and `uptime` are used to show the difference between running the pod/container manually and via `podman play `command. Notice the errors and much higher cpu load when running via `podman play kube`. The generated file you can see here: https://gist.github.com/tockar/9f6d4145709296803299fac444cfe9f7

```
❯ podman pod create --name testpod -p 8112:8112
86cb158d4dba63d9f0743ad244ba78608d5aa9fac7555e031cfe75bdc6626cda
❯ podman run --pod testpod -d --name deluge -e PUID=0 -e PGID=0 -e TZ=Europe/Paris -v ~/podman/deluge/config:/config -v ~/podman/deluge/downloads:/downloads linuxserver/deluge
dcb08128566bf3a5f4ec338c07a4bf731f757c838f39bd38385679c1ea0edc8b
❯ podman logs deluge | tail -n 5
[cont-init.d] 99-custom-scripts: exited 0.
[cont-init.d] done.
[services.d] starting services
[services.d] done.
23:28:22 [WARNING ][deluge.i18n.util           :83  ] IOError when loading translations: [Errno 2] No translation file found for domain: 'deluge'
❯ sleep 120; uptime
 23:30:53 up 1 day, 46 min,  1 user,  load average: 0,12, 0,25, 0,57
❯ podman generate kube testpod > bugreport.yml
❯ podman pod rm -af
86cb158d4dba63d9f0743ad244ba78608d5aa9fac7555e031cfe75bdc6626cda
❯ podman container rm -af
❯ podman play kube bugreport.yml
Trying to pull docker.io/linuxserver/deluge:latest...

8315c30c7d01d55d9cb33023b1a5d5aa017c61f1700c10a352b3133ca3debcc8

❯ podman logs testpod-deluge | tail -n 10
23:35:03 [WARNING ][deluge.i18n.util           :83  ] IOError when loading translations: [Errno 2] No translation file found for domain: 'deluge'
23:35:03 [ERROR   ][deluge.ui.web.web          :83  ] Couldn't listen on 0.0.0.0:8112: [Errno 98] Address already in use. 
Check that deluge-web or webui plugin is not already running.
Cannot run multiple daemons with same config directory.
If you believe this is an error, force starting by deleting: /config/deluged.pid
Cannot run multiple daemons with same config directory.
If you believe this is an error, force starting by deleting: /config/deluged.pid
23:35:06 [WARNING ][deluge.i18n.util           :83  ] IOError when loading translations: [Errno 2] No translation file found for domain: 'deluge'
23:35:06 [ERROR   ][deluge.ui.web.web          :83  ] Couldn't listen on 0.0.0.0:8112: [Errno 98] Address already in use. 
Check that deluge-web or webui plugin is not already running.
❯ sleep 120; uptime
 23:37:22 up 1 day, 52 min,  1 user,  load average: 2,01, 1,18, 0,83
```



**Output of `podman version`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
❯ apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unknown 2.2.1~4 amd64
podman/unknown,now 2.2.1~4 arm64 [installed]
podman/unknown 2.2.1~4 armhf
podman/unknown 2.2.1~4 s390x

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Raspberry Pi 4 4GB, Ubuntu 20.04, podman installed via the Kubic project.

comments.body:
its going to be hard replicating this with those volumes and mounts looking at your yaml -> https://gist.github.com/tockar/9f6d4145709296803299fac444cfe9f7

do you have a simpler yaml that shows the same problem? something like nginx or whatever?
@tockar poke, do you have a reproducer i could use?

Closing due to inactivity.

We can reopen once we have a reproducer.
